questions.question: What alert signal indicates immediate evacuation due to winds exceeding 50 MPH or tornado activity?
options: 2 long air horn blasts + radio communication
questions.explanation: 2 long air horn blasts plus radio communication signals the most severe weather condition requiring immediate evacuation.

questions.question: At what wind speed should audio truss be flattened to the roof?
questions.explanation: Winds of 40 MPH or greater require audio truss to be flattened to the roof with evacuation.

questions.question: Lightning within 8 miles requires all production areas to power off and take shelter.
questions.explanation: True. Lightning within 8 miles triggers show delays and requires all production areas to power off and take shelter.

questions.question: Which production areas should evacuate when winds exceed 50 MPH? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: All production areas should evacuate when winds exceed 50 MPH or during tornado activity.

questions.question: What is the appropriate response for LED screens when winds are nearing 30 MPH?
questions.explanation: When winds are nearing 30 MPH, LED screens should land PA and implement delays.

questions.question: When a storm is tracking towards the site within 20 miles, what should the lighting department do?
questions.explanation: When storms are tracking within 20 miles, lighting should have motor controllers at ready for quick response.

questions.question: Match the weather condition with the appropriate response:

pairs.definition: Show delayed, audience advised to seek shelter

questions.explanation: Each weather threshold has a specific alert method and response protocol.

questions.question: What should FOH (Front of House) do when winds are greater than 20 MPH?
questions.explanation: At 20+ MPH winds, FOH should secure gear and monitor conditions rather than immediately evacuating.

questions.question: Which conditions trigger the 'All Clear' signal? (Select all that apply)
options: All areas can resume normal activities
questions.explanation: The All Clear is signaled when weather is all clear and all areas can resume normal activities.

questions.question: Scenario: You are the production manager at an outdoor festival. Weather radar shows a storm with 45 MPH winds and lightning approaching within 10 miles. Walk through your decision-making process and actions for each production area.
questions.model_answer: 1) Issue radio alert to all departments. 2) Winds 40+ MPH: 1 long air horn blast - delay show, advise audience to seek shelter. 3) Stage: land anchor, message system. 4) LED: land PA and delays. 5) Audio: flatten truss to roof. 6) Lights: secure, power off, evacuate. 7) Spots/FOH/Video/Back Line: power off, evacuate. 8) Continue monitoring for lightning entering 8-mile radius which would trigger full shelter protocol.
